root_cause.description: The kafka-connector-v3.2 upgrade caused a consumer group rebalance that doubled the partition assignment from 3 to 6 partitions. Without deduplication configured, the pipeline consumed the same messages from previously-read partitions again, effectively processing each record twice and doubling the revenue aggregates.

contributing_factors.factor: No deduplication mechanism in the ETL pipeline
Kafka consumer group rebalance assigned all 6 partitions instead of previous 3
Connector upgrade changed partition assignment strategy
No data quality checks comparing input volume to historical baselines

causal_chain: New kafka-connector-v3.2 deployed during ETL window on March 27
Connector triggered consumer group rebalance on March 28
Pipeline assigned 6 partitions instead of previous 3
Records from partitions 0-2 were re-consumed from earlier offsets
Without deduplication, duplicate records flowed through the pipeline
Output row count stayed the same because aggregation grouped by key
